events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes torque-to-yield (TTY) bolts being reused, which is a defect since TTY bolts are one-time use only. This is a clear non-compliance and installed-defective condition.

  "findings": [
    "Torque-to-yield (TTY) bolts have been reused despite OEM one-time-use requirement",
    "TTY bolts are designed to stretch into the plastic deformation range on initial tightening; reuse risks insufficient clamp load and bolt failure",
    "Reused TTY fasteners may have reduced cross-section and compromised fatigue life"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and discard all reused TTY bolts",
    "Replace with new OEM-specified TTY bolts",
    "Re-torque to OEM specified procedure using the correct sequence and torque/yield specification"
  ],
  "rationale": "Torque-to-yield bolts are explicitly single-use fasteners. Reusing them is a direct violation of OEM/engineering specifications and creates a high risk of joint failure due to loss of clamp load or bolt fracture.",

    "OEM/engineering specs - torque-to-yield bolt one-time-use requirement"
  ]
